title: ‘I love you JB’: 6-year-old Celtics fan’s tearful message to Jaylen Brown goes viral
author: Emily Turner
bullets: 6-year-old Celtics fan Gio's tearful Instagram message to Jaylen Brown went viral following Brown's trade to the 76ers.
Gio's video, posted by his mother, expressed emotional support and gratitude for Brown, amassing over 840,000 likes.
Jaylen Brown responded to Gio's message with a comforting comment, assuring the young fan they would remain friends.

title: 4 things to know about Paul George, reportedly coming to the Celtics in the Jaylen Brown trade
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Paul George, 36, is reportedly joining the Boston Celtics in exchange for Jaylen Brown and draft picks.
George, a nine-time NBA All-Star, has averaged 20.5 points and 6.2 rebounds per game over his career.
Despite reaching several conference finals, George has never played in an NBA Finals during his 12 playoff appearances.

title: ‘To the people of Boston, thank you’: Jaylen Brown’s statement after reportedly being traded by the Celtics
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Jaylen Brown was reportedly traded from the Boston Celtics to the Philadelphia 76ers in a blockbuster deal.
Brown expressed mixed emotions in a statement, feeling both excited and disappointed about the trade.
He thanked Boston fans and expressed eagerness to earn respect in Philadelphia, marking a new chapter in his career.

title: Review & setlist: It was 100 degrees in Boston, and Goose was on fire
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Goose performed at Leader Bank Pavilion, Boston, on July 1, 2026, during a heatwave with 'feels like' temperatures reaching 102.
The band played mostly old favorites and only one track from their new album 'Big Modern!', showcasing funky jams and lighting effects.
Goose's setlist included 'Iguana Song', 'Electric Avenue', and a reggae-tinged encore, catering to a diverse audience of jam band fans.

title: Everything to know about July 4 in Boston (including how to watch the fireworks)
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: The 2026 Boston Pops Fireworks Spectacular will start earlier this year, with the concert at 7 p.m. and fireworks at 9:15 p.m.
Performers include Lainey Wilson, Chance The Rapper, and Megan Hilty, with Jane Lynch hosting and Keith Lockhart conducting.
Street closures will affect travel near the Esplanade; the MBTA will offer free rides starting at 8:30 p.m. on July 4.

title: In new podcast, former North Andover police officer Kelsey Fitzsimmons calls first judge ‘evil’ and recounts being shot
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Former officer Kelsey Fitzsimmons starts podcast recounting her shooting by a colleague and subsequent acquittal of assault charges.
Fitzsimmons speaks about postpartum depression and accuses North Andover police of manipulating the shooting narrative.
Fitzsimmons criticizes Judge Kathleen McCarthy-Neyman, describing her as 'evil' after being returned to jail post-initial release.

title: Bruins’ breakout prospect Cooper Simpson has a clear path to follow in Will Zellers
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Cooper Simpson and Will Zellers, both from Minnesota, are Boston Bruins prospects attending development camp together.
Simpson, a third-rounder in 2025, scored 34 goals with Youngstown Phantoms, demonstrating strong offensive skills.
Zellers was acquired in a trade and excelled in the USHL, earning Player of the Year with 44 goals for Green Bay.

title: New York woman indicted in alleged sex trafficking operation out of apartments in Allston, Brighton
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Zengzeng Liu, 40, was indicted for running a sex trafficking ring from Allston and Brighton brothels.
Liu allegedly recruited women from Asia and controlled their work conditions and appointments with clients.
Federal agents found nearly $105,000 in cash and evidence of sex acts in searches of the apartments.

title: ‘As a former Celtic, I do not approve’: Basketball world reacts to the Jaylen Brown trade
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Celtics traded Jaylen Brown to 76ers for Paul George and four draft picks, sparking strong reactions.
Former Boston center Kendrick Perkins disapproved of the trade calling it a 'sad day' for the Celtics.
National media expressed surprise and criticism; Jeff Goodman labeled it one of the worst trades seen.

title: A conversation with David Ortiz about the 2004 Red Sox, life as a baseball dad, and the current state of the team
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: David Ortiz reminisced about the Red Sox chemistry during their 2004 World Series victory at a Fanatics event in Boston.
Ortiz shared his experience as a baseball dad and the progress of his son D’Angelo's baseball career.
He encouraged patience with the current Red Sox, expressing optimism about the team's future performance.
